ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ
AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap converted to USD using the price of the token.

AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap converted to USD using the price of the token.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The Router is the Sender in the swap function.

TX_TO.name: TX_TO
TX_TO.comment: The tx_to is the address who receives the swapped token. This corresponds to the "to" field in the swap function.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol or platform that the liquidity pool belongs to or swap occurred on.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The address of the token sent for swap.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The address of the token being swapped to.

SYMBOL_IN.name: SYMBOL_IN
SYMBOL_IN.comment: The symbol of the token sent for swap.

SYMBOL_OUT.name: SYMBOL_OUT
SYMBOL_OUT.comment: The symbol of the token being swapped to.

EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID
EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

GAS_USED.comment: The gas used for this trace.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this trace. This is not necessarily the from address of the transaction.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_LIQUIDATIONS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a borrower's collateral asset is liquidated across various lending protocols. Liquidations occur when a borrower's health factor goes below 1 due to their collateral value not properly covering their loan/debt value. This might happen when the collateral decreases in value or the borrowed debt increases in value against each other. This collateral vs loan value ratio is shown in the health factor. In a liquidation, up to 50% of a borrower's debt is repaid and that value + liquidation fee is taken from the collateral available, so after a liquidation the amount liquidated from one's debt is repaid.

The origin addresses correspond to the to and from addresses in the `core.fact_transactions` table. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`.

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates bridge activity from event_logs, traces, and transfers, including bridge deposits and transfers. This table includes helpful columns, including an amount in USD where available. Note, this table only includes records for protocols with live, onchain bridge activity and may not represent the complete bridging picture.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address where the transaction originated from. This may be an EOA or contract address; however, in most cases, this is the user that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract responsible for handling the bridge deposit or transfer. This contract mediates the transfer and ensures that assets are sent and received appropriately.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The platform or protocol from which the bridge transaction or event originates.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The address that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer. This address is the sender of the tokens/assets being bridged to the destination chain. This may be an EOA or contract address.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the deposit or transfer. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER
DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the bridged tokens on the target chain after the completion of the bridge transaction. For non-evm chains, the hex address is decoded/encoded to match the data format of the destination chain, where possible. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain network to which the assets are being bridged. It could be any EVM compatible chain or other blockchain networks that the bridging protocol supports.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the destination blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address associated with the token that is being bridged. It provides a unique identifier for the token within its origin blockchain.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol representing the token being bridged. This provides a shorthand representation of the token.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The raw, non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the bridged tokens in USD at the time of the bridge transaction, where available.

EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

metadata.name: DIM_ASSETS
metadata.comment:
Table reports which assets are used during the batch interval, which can help identify periods of time of large activity for an asset. The table does not have a primary key and assets are duplicated in the table as they are used during different periods of time. To get a distinct count of assets on the network, please refer to the history_assets table. Table not widely used.

Learn more about Stellar assets: https://developers.stellar.org/docs/learn/fundamentals/stellar-data-structures/assets

ASSET_ID.name: ASSET_ID
ASSET_ID.comment: The Farm Hash encoding of Asset Code + Asset Issuer + Asset Type. This field is optimized for cross table joins since integer joins are less expensive than the original asset id components.

ASSET_TYPE.name: ASSET_TYPE
ASSET_TYPE.comment: The identifier for type of asset code, can be an alphanumeric with 4 characters, 12 characters or the native asset to the network, XLM.

ASSET_CODE.name: ASSET_CODE
ASSET_CODE.comment: The 4 or 12 character code representation of the asset on the network.

ASSET_ISSUER.name: ASSET_ISSUER
ASSET_ISSUER.comment: The account address of the original asset issuer that created the asset.

ID.name: ID
ID.comment: Numeric hash of asset_id, asset_type, and asset_issuer for join operations

DIM_ASSETS_ID.name: DIM_ASSETS_ID
DIM_ASSETS_ID.comment: Unique primary key for the dimension table, used as the main identifier for each record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was inserted.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when this record was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis. Stats for the current hour will be updated as new data arrives.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_TO_COUNT
UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.comment: The number of unique outputs keys to addresses in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.name: TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE
TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in the native fee currency.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: FACT_JETTON_METADATA
metadata.comment: Contains metadata information for Jettons (TEP-64 standard), including both on-chain and off-chain data.

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: Jetton master address.

UPDATE_TIMESTAMP_ONCHAIN.name: UPDATE_TIMESTAMP_ONCHAIN
UPDATE_TIMESTAMP_ONCHAIN.comment: Timestamp of the last on-chain update, converted from update_time_onchain.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: TEP-64 jetton symbol from on-chain, off-chain, or tonapi source.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: TEP-64 jetton decimals from on-chain, off-chain, or tonapi source. If not present, defaults to 9.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: TEP-64 jetton name from on-chain, off-chain, or tonapi source.

DESCRIPTION.name: DESCRIPTION
DESCRIPTION.comment: TEP-64 jetton description from on-chain, off-chain, or tonapi source.

IMAGE_DATA.name: IMAGE_DATA
IMAGE_DATA.comment: TEP-64 jetton image data from on-chain, off-chain, or tonapi source.

IMAGE.name: IMAGE
IMAGE.comment: TEP-64 jetton image URL from on-chain, off-chain, or tonapi source.

MINTABLE.name: MINTABLE
MINTABLE.comment: Boolean flag indicating if the jetton is mintable based on on-chain data.

JETTON_CONTENT_ONCHAIN.name: JETTON_CONTENT_ONCHAIN
JETTON_CONTENT_ONCHAIN.comment: JSON serialized string with on-chain jetton content.

JETTON_WALLET_CODE_HASH.name: JETTON_WALLET_CODE_HASH
JETTON_WALLET_CODE_HASH.comment: Jetton wallet code hash from on-chain data.

CODE_HASH.name: CODE_HASH
CODE_HASH.comment: Jetton code hash from on-chain data.

ADDING_AT.name: ADDING_AT
ADDING_AT.comment: Date when the output file was created.

SOURCES.name: SOURCES
SOURCES.comment: Record with sources of jetton metadata fields (symbol, name, description, image, image_data, decimals). Values can be 'onchain', 'offchain', 'tonapi', or '' (not set).

METADATA_STATUS.name: METADATA_STATUS
METADATA_STATUS.comment: Off-chain metadata update status: 0 - no off-chain metadata, 1 - success, -1 - error.

UPDATE_TIME_ONCHAIN.name: UPDATE_TIME_ONCHAIN
UPDATE_TIME_ONCHAIN.comment: Unix timestamp of on-chain update, such as admin address transfer.

UPDATE_TIME_METADATA.name: UPDATE_TIME_METADATA
UPDATE_TIME_METADATA.comment: Unix timestamp of off-chain metadata update.

FACT_JETTON_METADATA_ID.name: FACT_JETTON_METADATA_ID
FACT_JETTON_METADATA_ID.comment: Unique identifier for the jetton metadata record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when the record was inserted into the data warehouse.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: Timestamp when the record was last modified in the data warehouse.

CLOSED_AT.name: CLOSED_AT
CLOSED_AT.comment: Whether or not the asset is currently deleted from the user

COLLECTION_NFT.name: COLLECTION_NFT
COLLECTION_NFT.comment: TRUE/FALSE indicating if the NFT is part of a collection

ARC69_NFT.name: ARC69_NFT
ARC69_NFT.comment: TRUE/FALSE indicating if the NFT follows the ARC69 standard

AR3_NFT.name: AR3_NFT
AR3_NFT.comment: TRUE/FALSE indicating if the NFT follows the AR3 standard

TRADITIONAL_NFT.name: TRADITIONAL_NFT
TRADITIONAL_NFT.comment: TRUE/FALSE indicating if the asset total supply is 1 and the number of decimals is 0

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This table contains Deposit and Withdraw events from the coin module as well as  Deposit, Withdraw, DepositEvent, and WithdrawEvent from the fungible_asset module on the aptos blockchain. Note: transfers with a 0 amount are excluded.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

SUCCESS.name: SUCCESS
SUCCESS.comment: The boolean value indicating whether the transaction was successful or not.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: Unique identifier for the event. This is a monotonically increasing integer that is incremented for each event. This is useful for determining the order of changes.

CREATION_NUMBER.name: CREATION_NUMBER
CREATION_NUMBER.comment: Ceation number corresponding to the event stream originating from the given account.

TRANSFER_EVENT.name: TRANSFER_EVENT
TRANSFER_EVENT.comment: The type of transfer event. Value will either be  'WithdrawEvent' or 'DepositEvent'

ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The top level address for this event.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of a token. For example, if a token has 18 decimals, then the amount of 1 token is 10^18.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The full address of the token. This string contains the account,module, and resource.

IS_FUNGIBLE.name: IS_FUNGIBLE
IS_FUNGIBLE.comment: Boolean indicating whether the transfer was conducted using the legacy coin transfer mechanism (a simpler, original method for transferring coins) or the fungible_asset module (a newer, more flexible system for managing fungible assets).

FACT_TRANSFERS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSFERS_ID
FACT_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

bytes.description: Approximate size of the table as reported by Snowflake

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates bridge activity for bridge deposits and transfers sent from the following protocols: CELER, LAYER ZERO, MOVER, and WORMHOLE. Note, this table only includes records for the protocols listed above with live, onchain bridge activity and may not represent the complete bridging picture.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

VERSION.name: VERSION
VERSION.comment: The version also know as the height of the transactions that have been executed on the Aptos blockchain. The first transaction in the blockchain has a version of 0. The version number is incremented by 1 for each transaction that is executed on the blockchain.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The platform or protocol from which the bridge transaction or event originates.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract responsible for handling the bridge deposit or transfer. This contract mediates the transfer and ensures that assets are sent and received appropriately.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The third segment of the event type

TX_SENDER.name: TX_SENDER
TX_SENDER.comment: Sender is the address of the originator account for a transaction. A transaction must be signed by the originator.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The address that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer. This address is the sender of the tokens/assets being bridged to the destination chain.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the bridged tokens on the target chain after the completion of the bridge transaction. For non-evm chains, the hex address is decoded/encoded to match the data format of the destination chain, where possible.

SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.name: SOURCE_CHAIN_ID
SOURCE_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the source blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

SOURCE_CHAIN_NAME.name: SOURCE_CHAIN_NAME
SOURCE_CHAIN_NAME.comment: The name of the blockchain network from which the assets are being bridged.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the destination blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_NAME.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_NAME
DESTINATION_CHAIN_NAME.comment: The name of the blockchain network to which the assets are being bridged.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address associated with the token that is being bridged. It provides a unique identifier for the token within its origin blockchain.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of a token. For example, if a token has 18 decimals, then the amount of 1 token is 10^18.

FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

metadata.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This table contains a flattened easy version of the native transfers. This table uses the fact_transfers table as a base and filters down to only aptos tokens. The logic used to derive this table requires the withdrawal event to occur in the previous event to the deposit event and also requires the withdrawal event to be the same amount as the deposit event. The only exception to that rule is when a "CoinRegisterEvent" occurs in between the withdraw and deposit events. Any transfers that do not meet this criteria are not included in this table.

TO_ADDRESS.comment: The account address that received the transfer.

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for this EVM blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transaction fees and a value transferred in the native asset when applicable. Transactions may be native asset transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

Below are the specific native tokens that correspond to each EVM chain:

| Status     | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ETHEREUM   | ETH         |
| BINANCE    | BNB         |
| POLYGON    | POL         |
| AVALANCHE  | AVAX        |
| ARBITRUM   | ETH         |
| OPTIMISM   | ETH         |
| GNOSIS     | xDAI        |
| KAIA       | KLAY        |
| SEI        | SEI         |
| CORE       | CORE        |

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

metadata.name: EZ_SWAPS
metadata.comment: Deprecating Soon! Please migrate work to the new `defi.ez_dex_swaps` table by September 1st.

TX_ID.name: TX_ID
TX_ID.comment: ID for the transaction.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time for when the block was written.

BLOCK_HEIGHT.name: BLOCK_HEIGHT
BLOCK_HEIGHT.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

SWAP_CONTRACT.name: SWAP_CONTRACT
SWAP_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address of the DEX swap pool.
Note for metapier swaps in the labels table - they all appear to use the same master contract `A.609e10301860b683.PierPair` so the `pool_id` is essential to differentiating what pool is used in the swap route.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positioning of the swap within a transaction. The first swap will index at 0. A swap transaction may only run through 1 pool, in which case the max index will be 0. A multi-pool swap that runs through 2 pools, for example, will have 2 records with swap index 0 and 1.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The account address of the trader performing the swap action.

TOKEN_OUT_SOURCE.name: TOKEN_OUT_SOURCE
TOKEN_OUT_SOURCE.comment: The source address of the token out in the swap record. If this is the first token withdraw in a swap, then the address is likely to be the trader. This could also be the address of a pool if this swap index is later in the transaction.

TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT
TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped out of the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT
TOKEN_OUT_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is sending to the pool (selling).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_DESTINATION.name: TOKEN_IN_DESTINATION
TOKEN_IN_DESTINATION.comment: The destination address of the token in for the swap index. If this is the initial withdraw/deposit event interaction, it is likely to be the pool address for the swap. If this is the final destination for the token, this is likely to be the trader's address.

TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.comment:
The smart contract address for the token that is being swapped into the traders wallet.
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.name: TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT
TOKEN_IN_AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of the token being swapped that the trader is receiving from the pool (buying).
Token OUT / IN is from the perspective of the trader. So, an out token is leaving the wallet while the in token is being deposited to the wallet. This perspective is not changing for multi-pool trades - think of it directionally, the tokens continue flowing from out to in. Out of wallet, into pool, out of pool, into (another pool / wallet), with the final movement being tokens into final wallet.

EZ_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_SWAPS_ID
EZ_SWAPS_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for this EVM blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transaction fees and a value transferred in the native asset when applicable. Transactions may be native asset transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

Below are the specific native tokens that correspond to each EVM chain:

| Status     | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ETHEREUM   | ETH         |
| BINANCE    | BNB         |
| POLYGON    | POL         |
| AVALANCHE  | AVAX        |
| ARBITRUM   | ETH         |
| OPTIMISM   | ETH         |
| GNOSIS     | xDAI        |
| KAIA       | KLAY        |
| SEI        | SEI         |
| CORE       | CORE        |

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS

TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOKEN_STANDARD.name: TOKEN_STANDARD
TOKEN_STANDARD.comment: The type of token transferred in this event, for example `erc721`, `erc1155`, or `erc20`.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The name of the deployed contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol used to represent this contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The decimals for the token contract.

RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE
RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

RAW_AMOUNT.name: RAW_AMOUNT
RAW_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of tokens transferred. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The amount in US dollars for this transfer at the time of the transfer. Tokens without a decimal adjustment or price will be nulled out here.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_BLOCKS
metadata.comment: This table contains block level data for this EVM blockchain. This table can be used to analyze trends at a block level, for example gas fees vs. total transactions over time. For more information, please see [Etherscan Resources](https://etherscan.io/directory/Learning_Resources/Ethereum) or [The Ethereum Organization](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/blocks/)

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_HASH.name: BLOCK_HASH
BLOCK_HASH.comment: The hash of the block header for a given block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

NETWORK.name: NETWORK
NETWORK.comment: The network on the blockchain used by a transaction.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: This column is being removed for standardization purposes. Please remove from your queries by February 28, 2025.

DIFFICULTY.comment: The effort required to mine the block.

TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.name: TOTAL_DIFFICULTY
TOTAL_DIFFICULTY.comment: Total difficulty of the chain at a given block.

EXTRA_DATA.name: EXTRA_DATA
EXTRA_DATA.comment: Any data included by the miner for a given block.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT

PARENT_HASH.name: PARENT_HASH

RECEIPTS_ROOT.name: RECEIPTS_ROOT
RECEIPTS_ROOT.comment: The root of the state trie.

SHA3_UNCLES.name: SHA3_UNCLES
SHA3_UNCLES.comment: The mechanism which Ethereum Javascript RLP encodes an empty string.

SIZE.name: SIZE
SIZE.comment: Block size, which is determined by a given block's gas limit.

UNCLE_BLOCKS.name: UNCLE_BLOCKS
UNCLE_BLOCKS.comment: Uncle blocks occur when two blocks are mined and broadcasted at the same time, with the same block number. The block validated across the most nodes will be added to the primary chain, and the other one becomes an uncle block. Miners do receive rewards for uncle blocks.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
'For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/). This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for. The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters. You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query. The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
      
Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
```sql select  tx_hash, block_number, contract_address, decoded_log:from::string as from_address, decoded_log:to::string as to_address, decoded_log:value::integer as value from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48') and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000 and event_name = 'Transfer' limit 50```'

BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS
metadata.comment: This table contains details on decentralized exchange (DEX) liquidity pools (LP) on the kaia blockchain, including the tokens, symbols and decimals within each pool alongside the following protocols: DRAGONSWAP, KAIASWAP, KLAYSWAP, AND NEOPIN.

CREATION_BLOCK.name: CREATION_BLOCK
CREATION_BLOCK.comment: The block number of when this pool was created.

CREATION_TIME.name: CREATION_TIME
CREATION_TIME.comment: The block timestamp of when this pool was created.

CREATION_TX.name: CREATION_TX
CREATION_TX.comment: The transaction where this pool was created.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol or platform that the liquidity pool belongs to or swap occurred on.

FACTORY_ADDRESS.name: FACTORY_ADDRESS
FACTORY_ADDRESS.comment: The address that created or deployed this pool, where available.

POOL_ADDRESS.name: POOL_ADDRESS
POOL_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address for the liquidity pool.

POOL_NAME.name: POOL_NAME
POOL_NAME.comment: The name of the liquidity pool, where available. In some cases, the pool name is a concatenation of symbols or token addresses.

TOKENS.name: TOKENS
TOKENS.comment:
The address for the token included in the liquidity pool, as a JSON object. 

Query example to access the key:value pairing within the object:
SELECT
    DISTINCT pool_address AS unique_pools,
    tokens :token0 :: STRING AS token0,
    symbols: token0 :: STRING AS token0_symbol,
    decimals: token0 :: STRING AS token0_decimal
FROM kaia.defi.dim_dex_liquidity_pools
WHERE token0 = '0x98a8345bb9d3dda9d808ca1c9142a28f6b0430e1'
;

SYMBOLS.name: SYMBOLS
SYMBOLS.comment:
The symbol for the token included in the liquidity pool, as a JSON object, where available. 

Query example to access the key:value pairing within the object:
SELECT
    DISTINCT pool_address AS unique_pools,
    tokens :token0 :: STRING AS token0,
    symbols: token0 :: STRING AS token0_symbol,
    decimals: token0 :: STRING AS token0_decimal
FROM kaia.defi.dim_dex_liquidity_pools
WHERE token0_symbol = 'WETH'
;

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment:
The # of decimals for the token included in the liquidity pool, as a JSON object, where available. 

Query example to access the key:value pairing within the object:
SELECT
    DISTINCT pool_address AS unique_pools,
    tokens :token0 :: STRING AS token0,
    symbols: token0 :: STRING AS token0_symbol,
    decimals: token0 :: STRING AS token0_decimal
FROM kaia.defi.dim_dex_liquidity_pools
WHERE token0_decimal = 6
;

DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID.name: DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID
DIM_DEX_LIQUIDITY_POOLS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_PRICES_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table for determining token prices by address and blockchain, and native asset prices by symbol and blockchain. This data set is highly curated and contains metadata for one price per hour per unique asset and blockchain.

HOUR.name: HOUR
HOUR.comment: Hour that the price was recorded at.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The specific address representing the asset on a specific platform. This will be NULL if referring to a native asset. The case (upper / lower) may or may not be specified within the `dim_asset_metadata` table, as this column is raw and not transformed, coming directly from the provider APIs. However, in the `ez_` views, it will be lowercase by default for all EVMs.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol of asset.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimals for the asset. May be NULL.

PRICE.name: PRICE

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: The Blockchain, Network, or Platform for this asset.

IS_NATIVE.name: IS_NATIVE
IS_NATIVE.comment: A flag indicating assets native to the respective blockchain.

IS_IMPUTED.name: IS_IMPUTED
IS_IMPUTED.comment: A flag indicating if the price was imputed, or derived, from the last arriving record. This is generally used for tokens with low-liquidity or inconsistent reporting.

IS_DEPRECATED.name: IS_DEPRECATED
IS_DEPRECATED.comment: A flag indicating if the asset is deprecated or no longer supported by the provider.

BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number of this transaction.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp of this transaction.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the trace. Please note, this is not necessarily unique in this table as transactions frequently have multiple traces.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this trace. This is not necessarily the from address of the transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this trace. This is not necessarily the to address of the transaction.

INPUT.name: INPUT
INPUT.comment: The input data for this trace.

OUTPUT.name: OUTPUT
OUTPUT.comment: The output data for this trace.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The type of internal transaction. Common trace types are `CALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, and `STATICCALL`.

TRACE_ADDRESS.name: TRACE_ADDRESS
TRACE_ADDRESS.comment: The trace address for this trace.

SUB_TRACES.name: SUB_TRACES
SUB_TRACES.comment: The amount of nested sub traces for this trace.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The amount of the native asset transferred in this trace.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_HEX.name: VALUE_HEX
VALUE_HEX.comment: The value of the transaction in hexadecimal format.

GAS.name: GAS
GAS.comment: The gas supplied for this trace.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The gas used for this trace.

TRACE_SUCCEEDED.name: TRACE_SUCCEEDED
TRACE_SUCCEEDED.comment: The boolean value representing if the trace succeeded.

ERROR_REASON.name: ERROR_REASON
ERROR_REASON.comment: The reason for the trace failure, if any.

REVERT_REASON.name: REVERT_REASON
REVERT_REASON.comment: The reason for the revert, if available.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

IDENTIFIER.name: IDENTIFIER
IDENTIFIER.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please update your queries to use the `trace_address` column instead by February 28, 2025.

DATA.name: DATA

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

_LOG_ID.comment: This column is being removed for standardization purposes. Please remove or update your queries with `CONCAT(tx_hash, '_', event_index)` to reconstruct `_log_id` by February 28, 2025.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for this EVM blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transaction fees and a value transferred in the native asset when applicable. Transactions may be native asset transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

Below are the specific native tokens that correspond to each EVM chain:

| Status     | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ETHEREUM   | ETH         |
| BINANCE    | BNB         |
| POLYGON    | POL         |
| AVALANCHE  | AVAX        |
| ARBITRUM   | ETH         |
| OPTIMISM   | ETH         |
| GNOSIS     | xDAI        |
| KAIA       | KLAY        |
| SEI        | SEI         |
| CORE       | CORE        |

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_FEE.name: TX_FEE
TX_FEE.comment: Amount paid to validate the transaction in the native asset.

TX_FEE_PRECISE.name: TX_FEE_PRECISE
TX_FEE_PRECISE.comment: The precise amount of the transaction fee. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_TYPE.name: TX_TYPE
TX_TYPE.comment: The type of the transaction, 2 for EIP-1559 transactions and 0 for legacy transactions.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: The number of transactions sent from a given address.

INPUT_DATA.name: INPUT_DATA
INPUT_DATA.comment: This column contains additional data for this transaction, and is commonly used as part of a contract interaction or as a message to the recipient.

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE
GAS_PRICE.comment: Cost per unit of gas in Gwei.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: Gas used by the transaction.

EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.name: EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE
EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.comment: The effective gas price of the transaction, in wei.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Maximum amount of gas allocated for the transaction.

CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.name: CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED
CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.comment: The total amount of gas used when this transaction was executed in the block.

MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum fee per gas of the transaction, in wei.

MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum priority fee per gas of the transaction, in wei.

R.name: R
R.comment: The r value of the transaction signature.

S.name: S
S.comment: The s value of the transaction signature.

V.name: V
V.comment: The v value of the transaction signature.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

BLOCK_HASH.name: BLOCK_HASH
BLOCK_HASH.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please remove from your queries by February 28, 2025.

POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This will not be unique in this table as a transaction could include multiple transfer events.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: Contract address of the token being transferred.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE
RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

RAW_AMOUNT.name: RAW_AMOUNT
RAW_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of tokens transferred. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The amount in US dollars for this transfer at the time of the transfer. Tokens without a decimal adjustment or price will be nulled out here.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The number of decimal places this contract needs adjusted where token values exist. For example, use the decimal field to correctly transform raw amounts in ```fact_token_transfers```.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol belonging to the address of the token

EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID
EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

TOKEN_PRICE.name: TOKEN_PRICE
TOKEN_PRICE.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please remove from your queries by February 28, 2025.

HAS_DECIMAL.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please remove from your queries by February 28, 2025.

HAS_PRICE.name: HAS_PRICE
HAS_PRICE.comment: This column is being deprecated for standardization purposes. Please remove from your queries by February 28, 2025.

_LOG_ID.comment: This column is being removed for standardization purposes. Please remove or update your queries with `CONCAT(tx_hash, '_', event_index)` to reconstruct `_log_id` by February 28, 2025.

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: This column is being removed for standardization purposes. Please update your queries to use the `modified_timestamp` column instead by February 28, 2025.

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment:
This table currently contains swap events from the ```fact_event_logs``` table for DRAGONSWAP, KAIASWAP, KLAYSWAP, AND NEOPIN. along with other helpful columns including an amount USD where possible.
Note: A rule has been put in place to null out the amount_USD if that number is too divergent between amount_in_USD and amount_out_usd. This can happen for swaps of less liquid tokens during very high fluctuation of price.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The decoded event name for a given event.

AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ
AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: The value of the swapped tokens in USD at the time of the transaction, where available. If there is a variance of 75% or greater in USD swap values or one side of the swap's USD value is NULL, then both amount_in_usd and amount_out_usd will be set to NULL. This avoids falsely overstating USD swap volumes for low-liquidity token pairings or incorrectly reported prices. To get USD values in these scenarios anyway, please re-join the `price.ez_prices_hourly` table. Note, this logic does not apply for the chain's Native asset.

AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment: The value of the swapped tokens in USD at the time of the transaction, where available. If there is a variance of 75% or greater in USD swap values or one side of the swap's USD value is NULL, then both amount_in_usd and amount_out_usd will be set to NULL. This avoids falsely overstating USD swap volumes for low-liquidity token pairings or incorrectly reported prices. To get USD values in these scenarios anyway, please re-join the `price.ez_prices_hourly` table. Note, this logic does not apply for the chain's Native asset.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap converted to USD using the price of the token.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The Router is the Sender in the swap function.

TX_TO.name: TX_TO
TX_TO.comment: The tx_to is the address who receives the swapped token. This corresponds to the "to" field in the swap function.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol or platform that the liquidity pool belongs to or swap occurred on.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The address of the token sent for swap.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The address of the token being swapped to.

SYMBOL_IN.name: SYMBOL_IN
SYMBOL_IN.comment: The symbol of the token sent for swap.

SYMBOL_OUT.name: SYMBOL_OUT
SYMBOL_OUT.comment: The symbol of the token being swapped to.

_LOG_ID.name: _LOG_ID
_LOG_ID.comment: Deprecated. This column is no longer used. Please remove from your query by Jan. 31 2024.

EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID
EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS
TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.comment: The number of successful transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED
TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.comment: The number of failed transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_SIGNERS_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_SIGNERS_COUNT
UNIQUE_SIGNERS_COUNT.comment: The count of unique first signers for transactions in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.name: TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE
TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in Lamports.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID
EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: EZ_NATIVE_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains all transfers for the chain's native asset, sourced from internal traces (`core.fact_traces`), and includes decimal adjusted and USD values where available. The origin addresses correspond to the to and from addresses in the `core.fact_transactions` table. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: Native asset value transferred, in USD.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment:
This table contains swap events from the `fact_event_logs` table for popular decentralized exchanges (DEXes) on this EVM blockchain. It includes additional columns such as the amount in USD where possible. 
Note: A rule is applied to nullify the `amount_USD` if there is a significant divergence between `amount_in_USD` and `amount_out_usd`, which can occur during high price fluctuations for less liquid tokens.

TX_TO.name: TX_TO

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates bridge activity from event_logs, traces, and transfers, including bridge deposits and transfers. This table includes helpful columns, including an amount in USD where available. Note, this table only includes records for protocols with live, onchain bridge activity and may not represent the complete bridging picture.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address where the transaction originated from. This may be an EOA or contract address; however, in most cases, this is the user that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract responsible for handling the bridge deposit or transfer. This contract mediates the transfer and ensures that assets are sent and received appropriately.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The platform or protocol from which the bridge transaction or event originates.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The address that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer. This address is the sender of the tokens/assets being bridged to the destination chain. This may be an EOA or contract address.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the deposit or transfer. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER
DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the bridged tokens on the target chain after the completion of the bridge transaction. For non-evm chains, the hex address is decoded/encoded to match the data format of the destination chain, where possible. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain network to which the assets are being bridged. It could be any EVM compatible chain or other blockchain networks that the bridging protocol supports.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the destination blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address associated with the token that is being bridged. It provides a unique identifier for the token within its origin blockchain.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol representing the token being bridged. This provides a shorthand representation of the token.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The raw, non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the bridged tokens in USD at the time of the bridge transaction, where available.

EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction-related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN, and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.comment: The hour of the timestamp of the block.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN
BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.comment: The minimum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS
TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.comment: The number of successful transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED
TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.comment: The number of failed transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT
UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.comment: The number of unique origin from addresses in the hour.

UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_TO_COUNT
UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.comment: The number of unique origin to addresses in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.name: TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE
TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in the native asset. Note, Snowflake's native SUM() function may limit decimal precision. To view precise transaction fee values, please use the `tx_fee_precise` column in `core.fact_transactions`.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD, rounded to 2 decimal places.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID
EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table
