metadata.name: EZ_NFT_SALES
metadata.comment: This table contains NFT sale events from various marketplaces.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: The block number at which the NFT event occurred.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The block timestamp at which the NFT event occurred.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: The transaction hash for the NFT event. This is not necessarily unique in this table as a transaction may contain multiple NFT events.

EVENT_INDEX.name: EVENT_INDEX
EVENT_INDEX.comment: The event number within a transaction.

EVENT_TYPE.name: EVENT_TYPE
EVENT_TYPE.comment: The type of NFT event in this transaction, either sale, bid_won, redeem, or mint.

PLATFORM_ADDRESS.name: PLATFORM_ADDRESS
PLATFORM_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the exchange used for the transaction.

PLATFORM_NAME.name: PLATFORM_NAME
PLATFORM_NAME.comment: The name of the exchange used for the trade.

PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION.name: PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION
PLATFORM_EXCHANGE_VERSION.comment: The version of the exchange contract used for the transaction.

AGGREGATOR_NAME.name: AGGREGATOR_NAME
AGGREGATOR_NAME.comment: The name of the aggregator platform where the sale took place. If the sale did not take place via an aggregator platform, then the value will be null.

SELLER_ADDRESS.name: SELLER_ADDRESS
SELLER_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the seller of the NFT in the transaction.

BUYER_ADDRESS.name: BUYER_ADDRESS
BUYER_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the buyer of the NFT in the transaction.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the NFT used as collateral.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The name of the NFT project. This field, along with metadata, will be filled in over time.

TOKEN_ID.name: TOKEN_ID
TOKEN_ID.comment: The token ID for this NFT contract.

QUANTITY.name: QUANTITY
QUANTITY.comment: The number of NFTs transferred in this event by token_id. All ERC-721 tokens will have a value of 1 while ERC-1155 tokens may have a value of 1 or greater.

TOKEN_STANDARD.name: TOKEN_STANDARD
TOKEN_STANDARD.comment: The type of token transferred in this event, for example `erc721`, `erc1155`, or `erc20`.

CURRENCY_SYMBOL.name: CURRENCY_SYMBOL
CURRENCY_SYMBOL.comment: The token symbol for this NFT event.

CURRENCY_ADDRESS.name: CURRENCY_ADDRESS
CURRENCY_ADDRESS.comment: The token contract address for this NFT event. This will be the native asset for native transactions.

PRICE.name: PRICE
PRICE.comment: The total price of the NFT, in the currency in which the transaction occurred and decimal adjusted where possible. Please note that the price of the NFT, after subtracting total fees, may not represent the net amount paid to the seller in all cases. You may refer to the platform fee description for more info.

PRICE_USD.name: PRICE_USD
PRICE_USD.comment: The total price of the NFT in US dollars. This will be 0 for tokens without a decimal adjustment or hourly price. Please note that the price of the NFT, after subtracting total fees, may not represent the net amount paid to the seller in all cases. You may refer to the platform fee description for more info.

TOTAL_FEES.name: TOTAL_FEES
TOTAL_FEES.comment: The total amount of fees paid relating to the NFT purchase in the transaction currency. This includes royalty payments to creators and platform fees. Please note, this does not include the gas fee.

PLATFORM_FEE.name: PLATFORM_FEE
PLATFORM_FEE.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of fees paid to the platform for this NFT event in the transaction's currency. There are cases where there are fees paid to multiple marketplaces. In those cases, the fee in the platform_fee column will only reflect the platform fee related to the platform exchange contract.

CREATOR_FEE.name: CREATOR_FEE
CREATOR_FEE.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of fees paid to the NFT collection as royalty payments for this NFT event in the transaction's currency.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The total amount of fees paid relating to the NFT purchase in US dollars. This includes royalty payments to creators and platform fees. Please note, this does not include the gas fee.

PLATFORM_FEE_USD.name: PLATFORM_FEE_USD
PLATFORM_FEE_USD.comment: The amount of fees paid to the platform for this NFT event in US dollars. There are cases where there are fees paid to multiple marketplaces. In those cases, the fee in the platform_fee column will only reflect the platform fee related to the platform exchange contract.

CREATOR_FEE_USD.name: CREATOR_FEE_USD
CREATOR_FEE_USD.comment: The amount of fees paid to the NFT collection as royalty payments for this NFT event in US dollars.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address of this transaction. In most cases, this is the NFT buyer. However, for some more complex transactions, it may not be the NFT buyer.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address of this transaction. In most cases, this is the exchange contract. However, for some more complex NFT events, such as aggregate buys with tools, this may not be the exchange address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

EZ_NFT_SALES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for this EVM blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transaction fees and a value transferred in the native asset when applicable. Transactions may be native asset transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

Below are the specific native tokens that correspond to each EVM chain:

| Status     | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ETHEREUM   | ETH         |
| BINANCE    | BNB         |
| POLYGON    | POL         |
| AVALANCHE  | AVAX        |
| ARBITRUM   | ETH         |
| OPTIMISM   | ETH         |
| GNOSIS     | xDAI        |
| KAIA       | KLAY        |
| SEI        | SEI         |
| CORE       | CORE        |

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_FEE.name: TX_FEE
TX_FEE.comment: Amount paid to validate the transaction in the native asset.

TX_FEE_PRECISE.name: TX_FEE_PRECISE
TX_FEE_PRECISE.comment: The precise amount of the transaction fee. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: The number of transactions sent from a given address.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

INPUT_DATA.name: INPUT_DATA
INPUT_DATA.comment: This column contains additional data for this transaction, and is commonly used as part of a contract interaction or as a message to the recipient.

GAS_PRICE.name: GAS_PRICE
GAS_PRICE.comment: Cost per unit of gas in Gwei.

EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.name: EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE
EFFECTIVE_GAS_PRICE.comment: The total base charge plus tip paid for each unit of gas, in Gwei.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: Gas used by the transaction.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Maximum amount of gas allocated for the transaction.

CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.name: CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED
CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.comment: The total amount of gas used when this transaction was executed in the block.

MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum priority fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

Y_PARITY.name: Y_PARITY
Y_PARITY.comment: The y parity for the transaction.

ACCESS_LIST.name: ACCESS_LIST
ACCESS_LIST.comment: The access list for the transaction.

AUTHORIZATION_LIST.name: AUTHORIZATION_LIST
AUTHORIZATION_LIST.comment: Array of authorization entries (EIP-7702) containing information about contracts approved to act on behalf of the EOA.

R.name: R
R.comment: The r value of the transaction signature.

S.name: S
S.comment: The s value of the transaction signature.

V.name: V
V.comment: The v value of the transaction signature.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOPIC_0.name: TOPIC_0
TOPIC_0.comment: The first topic of the event, which is a unique identifier for the event.

TOPIC_1.name: TOPIC_1
TOPIC_1.comment: The second topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_2.name: TOPIC_2
TOPIC_2.comment: The third topic of the event, if applicable.

TOPIC_3.name: TOPIC_3
TOPIC_3.comment: The fourth topic of the event, if applicable.

EVENT_REMOVED.name: EVENT_REMOVED
EVENT_REMOVED.comment: Whether the event has been removed from the transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

FULL_DECODED_LOG.name: FULL_DECODED_LOG
FULL_DECODED_LOG.comment: The full decoded log, including the event name, the event parameters, and the data type of the event parameters.

DECODED_LOG.name: DECODED_LOG
DECODED_LOG.comment: The flattened decoded log, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.

CONTRACT_NAME.name: CONTRACT_NAME
CONTRACT_NAME.comment: The name of the contract, if the contract has a name() function.

EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

metadata.name: EZ_TOKEN_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: This convenience table contains emitted event logs for ERC-20 Token Transfers (e.g. `Transfer`: topic_0 = `0xddf252ad1be2c89b69c2b068fc378daa952ba7f163c4a11628f55a4df523b3ef`), including decimal adjusted values, USD values, and other helpful token metadata where available for each transfer event. The contract address is the token transferred, and the raw amount field is the amount of tokens transferred. Note, this table does not contain ERC-721 and ERC-1155 token transfers, instead please use `nft.ez_nft_transfers`. Additionally, this table does not contain transfers of the chain's native asset, instead please use `core.ez_native_transfers`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transfer.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transfer. This can be a contract address.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract that the event log was emitted from.

TOKEN_STANDARD.name: TOKEN_STANDARD
TOKEN_STANDARD.comment: The type of token transferred in this event, for example `erc721`, `erc1155`, or `erc20`.

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.comment: The name of the deployed contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The symbol used to represent this contract. Please note this is not necessarily unique.

DECIMALS.name: DECIMALS
DECIMALS.comment: The decimals for the token contract.

RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE
RAW_AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The amount of tokens transferred returned as a string to preserve precision. This value is not decimal adjusted.

RAW_AMOUNT.name: RAW_AMOUNT
RAW_AMOUNT.comment: The amount of tokens transferred. This value is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_PRECISE.name: AMOUNT_PRECISE
AMOUNT_PRECISE.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token returned as a string to preserve precision. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal transformed amount for this token. Tokens without a decimal adjustment will be nulled out here.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The amount in US dollars for this transfer at the time of the transfer. Tokens without a decimal adjustment or price will be nulled out here.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_REPAYMENTS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a borrower repays their loan (debt) across various lending protocols on this EVM blockchain. Loans are repaid in the same asset borrowed, plus accrued interest. Borrowers can pay back their loan based on the USD price as they can borrow any of the available stable coins (USDC, DAI, USDT, etc.). Some protocols required trace-level curation; as a result, certain columns exclusive to event logs, such as event index, will contain NULL values.

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates bridge activity from event_logs, traces, and transfers, including bridge deposits and transfers. This table includes helpful columns, including an amount in USD where available. Note, this table only includes records for protocols with live, onchain bridge activity and may not represent the complete bridging picture.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address where the transaction originated from. This may be an EOA or contract address; however, in most cases, this is the user that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the contract responsible for handling the bridge deposit or transfer. This contract mediates the transfer and ensures that assets are sent and received appropriately.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The platform or protocol from which the bridge transaction or event originates.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The address that initiated the bridge deposit or transfer. This address is the sender of the tokens/assets being bridged to the destination chain. This may be an EOA or contract address.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the deposit or transfer. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER
DESTINATION_CHAIN_RECEIVER.comment: The designated address set to receive the bridged tokens on the target chain after the completion of the bridge transaction. For non-evm chains, the hex address is decoded/encoded to match the data format of the destination chain, where possible. This may be an EOA or contract address.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The name of the blockchain network to which the assets are being bridged. It could be any EVM compatible chain or other blockchain networks that the bridging protocol supports.

DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID
DESTINATION_CHAIN_ID.comment: The numeric identifier associated with the destination blockchain network. This is specific to the chain and helps in uniquely identifying it.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address associated with the token that is being bridged. It provides a unique identifier for the token within its origin blockchain.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol representing the token being bridged. This provides a shorthand representation of the token.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The raw, non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the bridge transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the bridged tokens in USD at the time of the bridge transaction, where available.

EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_LIQUIDATIONS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a borrower's collateral asset is liquidated across various lending protocols. Liquidations occur when a borrower's health factor goes below 1 due to their collateral value not properly covering their loan/debt value. This might happen when the collateral decreases in value or the borrowed debt increases in value against each other. This collateral vs loan value ratio is shown in the health factor. In a liquidation, up to 50% of a borrower's debt is repaid and that value + liquidation fee is taken from the collateral available, so after a liquidation the amount liquidated from one's debt is repaid.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_FLASHLOANS
metadata.comment: This table contains flash loan transactions across various lending protocols. Flash loans are a feature designed for developers, due to the technical knowledge required to execute one. Flash Loans allow you to borrow any available amount of assets without providing any collateral, as long as the liquidity is returned to the protocol within one block transaction.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_DEPOSITS
metadata.comment: This table contains deposit transactions across various lending protocols on this EVM blockchain. A user deposits their preferred asset and amount. After depositing, users earn passive income based on the market borrowing demand. Additionally, depositing allows users to borrow by using their deposited assets as collateral. Any interest earned by depositing funds helps offset the interest rate accumulated by borrowing. Some protocols required trace-level curation; as a result, certain columns exclusive to event logs, such as event index, will contain NULL values.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

metadata.name: FACT_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened event logs from transactions on this EVM blockchain. Transactions may have multiple events, which are denoted by the event index for a transaction hash. Therefore, this table is unique on the combination of transaction hash and event index. Please see `fact_decoded_event_logs` or `ez_decoded_event_logs` for the decoded event logs.

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment:
This table contains swap events from the `fact_event_logs` table for popular decentralized exchanges (DEXes) on this EVM blockchain. It includes additional columns such as the amount in USD where possible. 
Note: A rule is applied to nullify the `amount_USD` if there is a significant divergence between `amount_in_USD` and `amount_out_usd`, which can occur during high price fluctuations for less liquid tokens.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ
AMOUNT_IN_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap converted to USD using the price of the token.

AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_OUT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap converted to USD using the price of the token.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The Router is the Sender in the swap function.

TX_TO.name: TX_TO
TX_TO.comment: The tx_to is the address who receives the swapped token. This corresponds to the "to" field in the swap function.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol or platform that the liquidity pool belongs to or swap occurred on.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The address of the token sent for swap.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The address of the token being swapped to.

SYMBOL_IN.name: SYMBOL_IN
SYMBOL_IN.comment: The symbol of the token sent for swap.

SYMBOL_OUT.name: SYMBOL_OUT
SYMBOL_OUT.comment: The symbol of the token being swapped to.

EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID
EZ_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: FACT_TRACES
metadata.comment: This table contains flattened trace data for internal contract calls. User-defined functions (UDFs) are available to decode hex encoded fields, including `utils.udf_hex_to_int()`.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

TRACE_INDEX.name: TRACE_INDEX
TRACE_INDEX.comment: The index of the trace within the transaction.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

INPUT.name: INPUT
INPUT.comment: The input data for this trace.

OUTPUT.name: OUTPUT
OUTPUT.comment: The output data for this trace.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.comment: The type of internal transaction. Common trace types are `CALL`, `DELEGATECALL`, and `STATICCALL`.

TRACE_ADDRESS.name: TRACE_ADDRESS
TRACE_ADDRESS.comment: The trace address for this trace.

SUB_TRACES.name: SUB_TRACES
SUB_TRACES.comment: The amount of nested sub traces for this trace.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_HEX.name: VALUE_HEX
VALUE_HEX.comment: The value of the transaction in hexadecimal format.

GAS.name: GAS
GAS.comment: The gas supplied for this trace.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The gas used for this trace.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this trace. This is not necessarily the from address of the transaction.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this trace. This is not necessarily the to address of the transaction.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the contract call that triggered this transaction.

TRACE_SUCCEEDED.name: TRACE_SUCCEEDED
TRACE_SUCCEEDED.comment: The boolean value representing if the trace succeeded.

ERROR_REASON.name: ERROR_REASON
ERROR_REASON.comment: The reason for the trace failure, if any.

REVERT_REASON.name: REVERT_REASON
REVERT_REASON.comment: The reason for the revert, if available.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

FACT_TRACES_ID.name: FACT_TRACES_ID
FACT_TRACES_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction-related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN, and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.comment: The hour of the timestamp of the block.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN
BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.comment: The minimum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block number in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS
TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.comment: The number of successful transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED
TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.comment: The number of failed transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT
UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.comment: The number of unique origin from addresses in the hour.

UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_TO_COUNT
UNIQUE_TO_COUNT.comment: The number of unique origin to addresses in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.name: TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE
TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in the native asset. Note, Snowflake's native SUM() function may limit decimal precision. To view precise transaction fee values, please use the `tx_fee_precise` column in `core.fact_transactions`.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD, rounded to 2 decimal places.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID
EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment:
This table contains transaction level data for this EVM blockchain. Each transaction will have a unique transaction hash, along with transaction fees and a value transferred in the native asset when applicable. Transactions may be native asset transfers or interactions with contract addresses. For more information, please see [The Ethereum Organization - Transactions](https://ethereum.org/en/developers/docs/transactions/)

Below are the specific native tokens that correspond to each EVM chain:

| Status     | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ETHEREUM   | ETH         |
| BINANCE    | BNB         |
| POLYGON    | POL         |
| AVALANCHE  | AVAX        |
| ARBITRUM   | ETH         |
| OPTIMISM   | ETH         |
| GNOSIS     | xDAI        |
| KAIA       | KLAY        |
| SEI        | SEI         |
| CORE       | CORE        |

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed.

FROM_ADDRESS.name: FROM_ADDRESS
FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The sending address of this transaction.

TO_ADDRESS.name: TO_ADDRESS
TO_ADDRESS.comment: The receiving address of this transaction. This can be a contract address.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of the call that triggered this transaction.

VALUE.name: VALUE
VALUE.comment: The value transacted in the native asset.

VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.name: VALUE_PRECISE_RAW
VALUE_PRECISE_RAW.comment: The precise, unadjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

VALUE_PRECISE.name: VALUE_PRECISE
VALUE_PRECISE.comment: The precise, adjusted amount of the transaction. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_FEE.name: TX_FEE
TX_FEE.comment: Amount paid to validate the transaction in the native asset.

TX_FEE_PRECISE.name: TX_FEE_PRECISE
TX_FEE_PRECISE.comment: The precise amount of the transaction fee. This is returned as a string to avoid precision loss.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Whether the transaction was successful, returned as a boolean.

TIMEBOOSTED.name: TIMEBOOSTED
TIMEBOOSTED.comment: Whether the transaction was time boosted (Arbitrum specific).

NONCE.name: NONCE
NONCE.comment: The number of transactions sent from a given address.

TX_POSITION.name: TX_POSITION
TX_POSITION.comment: The position of the transaction within the block.

INPUT_DATA.name: INPUT_DATA
INPUT_DATA.comment: This column contains additional data for this transaction, and is commonly used as part of a contract interaction or as a message to the recipient.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: Gas used by the transaction.

GAS_LIMIT.name: GAS_LIMIT
GAS_LIMIT.comment: Maximum amount of gas allocated for the transaction.

CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.name: CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED
CUMULATIVE_GAS_USED.comment: The total amount of gas used when this transaction was executed in the block.

MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.name: MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS
MAX_PRIORITY_FEE_PER_GAS.comment: The maximum priority fee per gas of the transaction, in Gwei.

Y_PARITY.name: Y_PARITY
Y_PARITY.comment: The y parity for the transaction.

ACCESS_LIST.name: ACCESS_LIST
ACCESS_LIST.comment: The access list for the transaction.

R.name: R
R.comment: The r value of the transaction signature.

S.name: S
S.comment: The s value of the transaction signature.

V.name: V
V.comment: The v value of the transaction signature.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a user withdraws liquidity across various lending protocols on this EVM blockchain. Users need to make sure there is enough liquidity (not borrowed) in order to withdraw, if this is not the case, users need to wait for more liquidity from depositors or borrowers repaying. Some protocols required trace-level curation; as a result, certain columns exclusive to event logs, such as event index, will contain NULL values.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated when a transaction is executed. This field will not be unique in this table, as a given transaction can include multiple events.

CONTRACT_ADDRESS.name: CONTRACT_ADDRESS
CONTRACT_ADDRESS.comment: The address interacted with for a given event.

EVENT_NAME.name: EVENT_NAME
EVENT_NAME.comment: The name of the event, as defined in the contract ABI.

ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.name: ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE
ORIGIN_FUNCTION_SIGNATURE.comment: The function signature of this transaction.

ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_FROM_ADDRESS.comment: The from address at the transaction level.

ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.name: ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS
ORIGIN_TO_ADDRESS.comment: The to address at the transaction level.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The specific protocol where the lending event occurred.

DEPOSITOR.name: DEPOSITOR
DEPOSITOR.comment: It's the address of the user who is borrowing or repaying the loan, depending on the action.

PROTOCOL_MARKET.name: PROTOCOL_MARKET
PROTOCOL_MARKET.comment: The protocol's specific lending asset token.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the token associated with the lending action.

TOKEN_SYMBOL.name: TOKEN_SYMBOL
TOKEN_SYMBOL.comment: The symbol of the token associated with the lending action.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: The non-decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The decimal adjusted amount of tokens involved in the lending transaction, where available.

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: The value of the tokens in USD at the time of the lending transaction, where available.

EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS_ID.name: EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS_ID
EZ_LENDING_WITHDRAWS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The UTC timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: EZ_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: DEX Swaps on the NEAR blockchain, with label and price data joined in for ease of use (where available). Note - view updated February 2024 to use the same v2 Swaps model as fact_dex_swaps. TOKEN_IN and TOKEN_OUT are now SYMBOL_IN/OUT. POOL ID is found in the SWAP_INPUT_DATA, where available.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Unique identifier (hash) of this transaction.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol utilized for the operation, likely a swap or bridge.

POOL_ID.name: POOL_ID
POOL_ID.comment: The unique id for the pool involved in the swap.

TRADER.name: TRADER
TRADER.comment: The address who initiated the swap.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The index number of the swap indicating its order of execution in the transaction.

AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_OUT_RAW
AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_OUT.name: AMOUNT_OUT
AMOUNT_OUT.comment: The amount of tokens taken out of or received from the swap.

AMOUNT_OUT_USD.name: AMOUNT_OUT_USD
AMOUNT_OUT_USD.comment: Token amount for the record decimal adjusted and multiplied by a price in USD, if available.

TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT
TOKEN_OUT_CONTRACT.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

SYMBOL_OUT.name: SYMBOL_OUT
SYMBOL_OUT.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

AMOUNT_IN_RAW.name: AMOUNT_IN_RAW
AMOUNT_IN_RAW.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_IN.name: AMOUNT_IN
AMOUNT_IN.comment: The amount of tokens put into the swap.

AMOUNT_IN_USD.name: AMOUNT_IN_USD
AMOUNT_IN_USD.comment: Token amount for the record decimal adjusted and multiplied by a price in USD, if available.

TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.name: TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT
TOKEN_IN_CONTRACT.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

SYMBOL_IN.name: SYMBOL_IN
SYMBOL_IN.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

SWAP_INPUT_DATA.name: SWAP_INPUT_DATA
SWAP_INPUT_DATA.comment:
JSON of input data for the swap, taken from the FunctionCall JSON.
Note, directional keys such as in and out are determined by the pool.

LOG.name: LOG

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

metadata.name: LOGS_S3
metadata.comment: This table extracts all logs from receipts and decodes the arguments for easy use.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Unique identifier (hash) of this transaction.

RECEIPT_ID.name: RECEIPT_ID
RECEIPT_ID.comment: The identifying hash for a receipt.

LOG_ID.name: LOG_ID
LOG_ID.comment: Concatenation between receipt id and index of the log.

LOG_INDEX.name: LOG_INDEX
LOG_INDEX.comment: The positon index for the record in question, if it was stored in a particular order.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

PREDECESSOR_ID.name: PREDECESSOR_ID
PREDECESSOR_ID.comment:
This is the account that was the immediate caller to the smart contract. If this is a simple transaction (no cross-contract calls) from alice.near to banana.near, the smart contract at banana.near considers Alice the predecessor. In this case, Alice would also be the signer.  
  
Source: https://docs.near.org/tutorials/crosswords/beginner/actions#predecessor-signer-and-current-account

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

CLEAN_LOG.name: CLEAN_LOG
CLEAN_LOG.comment: The logs linked to the transaction.

IS_STANDARD.name: IS_STANDARD
IS_STANDARD.comment: A boolean value indicating whether it is a standardized log or not.

GAS_BURNT.name: GAS_BURNT
GAS_BURNT.comment:
The gas used in the object, whether it be the transaction, action or receipt. In the case of a curated model where this column is included alongside a transaction fee, this is the gas burnt during the individual action or receipt.

In raw number format with 16 decimal places, to adjust divide by POW(10,16) or multiply by 1e-16.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.name: _PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER
_PARTITION_BY_BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Block number grouping for ingestion partition

_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: _INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
_INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp for when the block was inserted into the data warehouse by the Chainwalker.

LOGS_ID.name: LOGS_ID
LOGS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

_INVOCATION_ID.name: _INVOCATION_ID
_INVOCATION_ID.comment: A job ID to identify the run that last modified a record.

metadata.name: FACT_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: Modeled bridge activity, tracking tokens through the NEAR Ecosystem.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Unique identifier (hash) of this transaction.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.name: DESTINATION_ADDRESS
DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: The address tokens are being sent to, in a transfer or bridge transaction.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment:
The originating address from which tokens are being sent, in a transfer or bridge transaction.  

This addressed is ascertained as best as possible, but may be null depending on the available information.  

Example 1 - tx CzPRdoiU74bo9yqqt9fCP5Xdab2mPVqxr9gU5xeaQA4a stores just "signer_public_key 0x0x1589…f694" on chain.  

Example 2 - tx FA7tQeGQnhminbas28YgaizwmT4vnEUiCr3vdXmN3Xjn does not log any signing address at all.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol utilized for the operation, likely a swap or bridge.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The recipient blockchain in a bridge transaction.

SOURCE_CHAIN.name: SOURCE_CHAIN

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION

metadata.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY
metadata.comment: Modeled bridge activity, tracking tokens through the NEAR Ecosystem. Token labels and USD Price appended to record, where available.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Unique identifier (hash) of this transaction.

TOKEN_ADDRESS.name: TOKEN_ADDRESS
TOKEN_ADDRESS.comment: The contract address corresponding to the token, view details of the contract by following this address on the chain explorer.

AMOUNT_UNADJ.name: AMOUNT_UNADJ
AMOUNT_UNADJ.comment: An unadjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.) for the relevant record. This is the number as it appears on chain and is not decimal adjusted.

AMOUNT_ADJ.name: AMOUNT_ADJ
AMOUNT_ADJ.comment: A decimal adjusted amount (of tokens, price, etc.).

SYMBOL.name: SYMBOL
SYMBOL.comment: The shorthand symbol for the token, as used by common price feeds and exchanges.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment:
The amount of tokens transferred
If in the ez_ view, this number is decimal adjusted to actual amount. If in silver level or a fact_ view, the raw amount is presented

AMOUNT_USD.name: AMOUNT_USD
AMOUNT_USD.comment: Token amount for the record decimal adjusted and multiplied by a price in USD, if available.

DESTINATION_ADDRESS.name: DESTINATION_ADDRESS
DESTINATION_ADDRESS.comment: The address tokens are being sent to, in a transfer or bridge transaction.

SOURCE_ADDRESS.name: SOURCE_ADDRESS
SOURCE_ADDRESS.comment:
The originating address from which tokens are being sent, in a transfer or bridge transaction.  

This addressed is ascertained as best as possible, but may be null depending on the available information.  

Example 1 - tx CzPRdoiU74bo9yqqt9fCP5Xdab2mPVqxr9gU5xeaQA4a stores just "signer_public_key 0x0x1589…f694" on chain.  

Example 2 - tx FA7tQeGQnhminbas28YgaizwmT4vnEUiCr3vdXmN3Xjn does not log any signing address at all.

PLATFORM.name: PLATFORM
PLATFORM.comment: The protocol utilized for the operation, likely a swap or bridge.

BRIDGE_ADDRESS.name: BRIDGE_ADDRESS
BRIDGE_ADDRESS.comment: The address of the deployed contract for the token, nft, or other smart contract.

DESTINATION_CHAIN.name: DESTINATION_CHAIN
DESTINATION_CHAIN.comment: The recipient blockchain in a bridge transaction.

SOURCE_CHAIN.name: SOURCE_CHAIN
SOURCE_CHAIN.comment: The originating blockchain in a bridge transaction.

METHOD_NAME.name: METHOD_NAME
METHOD_NAME.comment: Name of the method(s) approved for use. NULL if permission is FullAccess.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.comment:
Direction of funds through a bridge. Inbound = tokens bridged to Near ecosystem.

Note - for the Rainbow bridge, inbound means Near or Aurora as tokens pass through Near to get to Aurora.

RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.name: RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED
RECEIPT_SUCCEEDED.comment: A boolean indicating whether the receipt was successfully processed, based on the presence of a Failure message.

EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.name: EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID
EZ_BRIDGE_ACTIVITY_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS
metadata.comment: Parses log output data for swap information. It was determined logs must be used over inputs in a FunctionCall as only the output contains actual swap information. See tx AfvgkUxP8taJNBLaZYvFumFrrePpJujb2gjQJz7YbRiM as an example.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH
TX_HASH.comment: Unique identifier (hash) of this transaction.

RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.name: RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID
RECEIPT_OBJECT_ID.comment: Please note this column is being deprecated in favor of simply "receipt_id".

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The height of the chain this block corresponds with.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

RECEIVER_ID.name: RECEIVER_ID
RECEIVER_ID.comment:
The address of the NEAR account that the receipt was executed on.
Note, this differs from `tx_receiver` and is associated with the receipt within a transaction. It is often a contract on which a method is executed by the `signer_id`.

SIGNER_ID.name: SIGNER_ID
SIGNER_ID.comment: The signer is the account that originally signed the transaction that began the blockchain activity, which may or may not include cross-contract calls. If a function calls results in several cross-contract calls, think of the signer as the account that pushed over the first domino in that chain reaction.

SWAP_INDEX.name: SWAP_INDEX
SWAP_INDEX.comment: The positon index for the record in question, if it was stored in a particular order.

AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.name: AMOUNT_OUT_RAW
AMOUNT_OUT_RAW.comment: Amount out, in a swap or transfer, in adjusted form.

TOKEN_OUT.name: TOKEN_OUT
TOKEN_OUT.comment: The address of the token being swapped to.

AMOUNT_IN_RAW.name: AMOUNT_IN_RAW
AMOUNT_IN_RAW.comment: Amount in, in a swap or transfer, in adjusted form.

TOKEN_IN.name: TOKEN_IN
TOKEN_IN.comment: The address of the token sent for swap.

SWAP_INPUT_DATA.name: SWAP_INPUT_DATA
SWAP_INPUT_DATA.comment:
JSON of input data for the swap, taken from the FunctionCall JSON.
Note, directional keys such as in and out are determined by the pool.

LOG.name: LOG
LOG.comment: The logs linked to the transaction.

FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.name: FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID
FACT_DEX_SWAPS_ID.comment: A unique identifier for the record.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

clustering_key.description: The key used to cluster this table

row_count.description: An approximate count of rows in this table

metadata.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY
metadata.comment: A convenience table that aggregates block and transaction related metrics using various aggregate functions such as SUM, COUNT, MIN and MAX from the fact_transactions table, on an hourly basis. Stats for the current hour will be updated as new data arrives.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_HOUR.comment: The hour of the timestamp of the block.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN
BLOCK_NUMBER_MIN.comment: The minimum block id in the hour.

BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.name: BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX
BLOCK_NUMBER_MAX.comment: The maximum block id in the hour.

BLOCK_COUNT.name: BLOCK_COUNT
BLOCK_COUNT.comment: The number of blocks in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT
TRANSACTION_COUNT.comment: The number of transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS
TRANSACTION_COUNT_SUCCESS.comment: The number of successful transactions in the hour.

TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.name: TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED
TRANSACTION_COUNT_FAILED.comment: The number of failed transactions in the hour.

UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.name: UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT
UNIQUE_FROM_COUNT.comment: The number of unique tx_from addresses in the hour.

TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.name: TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE
TOTAL_FEES_NATIVE.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in the native fee currency.

TOTAL_FEES_USD.name: TOTAL_FEES_USD
TOTAL_FEES_USD.comment: The sum of all fees in the hour, in USD.

EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.name: EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID
EZ_CORE_METRICS_HOURLY_ID.comment: A uniquely generated identifier assigned by a surrogate key

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which the record was initially created and inserted into this table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp at which this record was last modified by an internal process.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

TEST_TIMESTAMP.comment: The timestamp of the observability test run.

metadata.name: FACT_VALIDATORS
metadata.comment: A table that contains labels for and additional information about Cosmos Hub validators

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS
ADDRESS.comment: Address unique to an individual wallet, validator, or token.

BLOCKCHAIN.name: BLOCKCHAIN
BLOCKCHAIN.comment: In this table, always Cosmos. Used to join to cross-chain tables.

CREATOR.name: CREATOR
CREATOR.comment: Name of the label creator - for now, this will always be "Flipside."

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE
LABEL_TYPE.comment: A broad category that describes what a label is representing.

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE
LABEL_SUBTYPE.comment: Adds more detail to the label type.

LABEL.name: LABEL
LABEL.comment: The label or name of the address.

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the project the label belongs to.

ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.name: ACCOUNT_ADDRESS
ACCOUNT_ADDRESS.comment: The Cosmos address of the user / entity that operates the validator.

DELEGATOR_SHARES.name: DELEGATOR_SHARES
DELEGATOR_SHARES.comment: The number of tokens staked to the validator.

SELF_DELEGATION.name: SELF_DELEGATION
SELF_DELEGATION.comment: The number of ATOM tokens that the operator currently has staked with their own validator.

NUM_DELEGATORS.name: NUM_DELEGATORS
NUM_DELEGATORS.comment: The number of wallets that stake their ATOM tokens with the validator.

RATE.name: RATE
RATE.comment: The commission rate that the validator is currently charging.

MAX_CHANGE_RATE.name: MAX_CHANGE_RATE
MAX_CHANGE_RATE.comment: The maximum rate at which a validator can change their commission per day.

MAX_RATE.name: MAX_RATE
MAX_RATE.comment: The maximum commission rate that the validator can charge

COMMISSION_RATE_LAST_UPDATED.name: COMMISSION_RATE_LAST_UPDATED
COMMISSION_RATE_LAST_UPDATED.comment: The date of the last commission change.

STATUS.name: STATUS
STATUS.comment: Validators with a status of BOND_STATUS_BONDED are active and validators with a status of BOND_STATUS_UNBONDED are inactive.

JAILED.name: JAILED
JAILED.comment: FALSE when a validator is not jailed, TRUE when a validator is jailed

RANK.name: RANK
RANK.comment: The rank of the validator in the validator set. Rank is determined by the number of ATOM tokens staked to the validator

NUM_GOVERNANCE_VOTES.name: NUM_GOVERNANCE_VOTES
NUM_GOVERNANCE_VOTES.comment: The number of governance proposals the validator has voted on during its lifetime.

RAW_METADATA.name: RAW_METADATA
RAW_METADATA.comment: Additional details about the validator or token in json format.

UNIQUE_KEY.name: UNIQUE_KEY
UNIQUE_KEY.comment: The unique key of the table

FACT_VALIDATORS_ID.name: FACT_VALIDATORS_ID
FACT_VALIDATORS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

ADDRESS.name: ADDRESS

CREATOR.name: CREATOR

LABEL_TYPE.name: LABEL_TYPE

LABEL_SUBTYPE.name: LABEL_SUBTYPE

LABEL.name: LABEL

PROJECT_NAME.name: PROJECT_NAME
PROJECT_NAME.comment: The name of the project the label belongs to.

ALIAS.name: ALIAS
ALIAS.comment: A secondary address for the token, where available

DECIMAL.name: DECIMAL
DECIMAL.comment: Divide amount by decimal to get the actual amount of currency being transferred.

RAW_METADATA.name: RAW_METADATA
RAW_METADATA.comment: Additional details about the validator or token in json format.

UNIQUE_KEY.name: UNIQUE_KEY
UNIQUE_KEY.comment: The unique key of the table

DIM_TOKENS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSFERS
metadata.comment: Records of all transfers on Cosmos from chain version 4 and beyond. (This info may be available in the msgs column in core.fact_transactions, but we are not parsing it out at this time.)

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TX_ID.name: TX_ID
TX_ID.comment: A unique key that identifies a transaction. Called "TxHash" on block explorers.

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status is "TRUE" if the transaction went through, "FALSE" if the transaction failed.

TRANSFER_TYPE.name: TRANSFER_TYPE
TRANSFER_TYPE.comment: Details on the type of transfer occurring during the transaction. "Transfer_In" = depositing tokens onto the Cosmos Hub. "Transfer_out" = withdrawing tokens from the Cosmos Hub. "Cosmos" = wallet to wallet transfer on the Cosmos Hub.

SENDER.name: SENDER
SENDER.comment: The wallet address of the individual sent tokens in the transfer.

AMOUNT.name: AMOUNT
AMOUNT.comment: The amount that was used in the transaction message.

CURRENCY.name: CURRENCY
CURRENCY.comment: The currency that was used in the transaction message.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER
RECEIVER.comment: The wallet address of the individual received tokens in the transfer.

UNIQUE_KEY.name: UNIQUE_KEY
UNIQUE_KEY.comment: The unique key for the table.

FACT_TRANSFERS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSFERS_ID
FACT_TRANSFERS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment: Records of all transactions that have occurred on Cosmos, dating back to the genesis block. NOTE - FOR CHAIN VERSIONS 1-3 THE TX_FROM, FEE, AND FEE_DENOM COLUMNS ARE ALWAYS NULL. (This info may be available in the msgs column, but we are not parsing it out at this time.)

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TX_ID.name: TX_ID
TX_ID.comment: A unique key that identifies a transaction. Called "TxHash" on block explorers.

TX_FROM.name: TX_FROM
TX_FROM.comment: The wallet address of the individual who initiated the transaction

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status is "TRUE" if the transaction went through, "FALSE" if the transaction failed.

FEE.name: FEE
FEE.comment: The fee is paid by the initiator of the transaction. Fee = gas * gas price.

FEE_DENOM.name: FEE_DENOM
FEE_DENOM.comment: The denominator of the transaction fee.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The amount of gas consumed by the transaction.

GAS_WANTED.name: GAS_WANTED
GAS_WANTED.comment: Amount of gas requested for a transaction. It is provided by users when the transaction is generated.

TX_CODE.name: TX_CODE
TX_CODE.comment: A number that corresponds to various error codes. When "0", the transaction is successful. Non-zero numbers signify different types of transaction failures.

TX_LOG.name: TX_LOG
TX_LOG.comment: A string that contains the transaction logs, which are logs written by the program interacted with during the transaction.

MSGS.name: MSGS
MSGS.comment: The underlying json from the messages or events within the transactions

UNIQUE_KEY.name: UNIQUE_KEY
UNIQUE_KEY.comment: The unique key for the table.

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: BLOCKS_COMPLETENESS
metadata.comment: Records of all blocks block gaps (missing blocks) with a timestamp the test was run

MIN_BLOCK.name: MIN_BLOCK
MIN_BLOCK.comment: The lowest block id in the test

MAX_BLOCK.name: MAX_BLOCK
MAX_BLOCK.comment: The highest block id in the test

MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
MIN_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The lowest block timestamp in the test

MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
MAX_BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The highest block timestamp in the test

DECIMAL.comment: Divide the raw amount of currency as provided in the amount column by the decimal to get the actual amount in AXL or other currency.

RECEIVER.name: RECEIVER

FOREIGN_ADDRESS.name: FOREIGN_ADDRESS

metadata.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS
metadata.comment: Records of all transactions that have occurred on Axelar, dating back to the genesis block.

BLOCK_ID.name: BLOCK_ID
BLOCK_ID.comment: The block height the block was recorded at.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block began.

TX_ID.name: TX_ID
TX_ID.comment: A unique key that identifies a transaction. Called "TxHash" on block explorers.

TX_FROM.name: TX_FROM
TX_FROM.comment: The wallet address of the individual who initiated the transaction

TX_SUCCEEDED.name: TX_SUCCEEDED
TX_SUCCEEDED.comment: Transaction status is "TRUE" if the transaction went through, "FALSE" if the transaction failed.

FEE.name: FEE
FEE.comment: The fee is paid by the initiator of the transaction. Fee = gas * gas price and is given in micro-AXL.

FEE_DENOM.name: FEE_DENOM
FEE_DENOM.comment: The denominator of the transaction fee.

GAS_USED.name: GAS_USED
GAS_USED.comment: The amount of gas consumed by the transaction.

GAS_WANTED.name: GAS_WANTED
GAS_WANTED.comment: Amount of gas requested for a transaction. It is provided by users when the transaction is generated.

TX_CODE.name: TX_CODE
TX_CODE.comment: A number that corresponds to various error codes. When "0", the transaction is successful. Non-zero numbers signify different types of transaction failures.

TX_LOG.name: TX_LOG
TX_LOG.comment: A string that contains the transaction logs, which are logs written by the program interacted with during the transaction.

MSGS.name: MSGS
MSGS.comment: The underlying json from the messages or events within the transactions

FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.name: FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID
FACT_TRANSACTIONS_ID.comment: The unique identifier for each row in the table.

INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.name: INSERTED_TIMESTAMP
INSERTED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was inserted into the table.

MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.name: MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP
MODIFIED_TIMESTAMP.comment: The utc timestamp at which the row was last modified.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_FLASHLOANS
metadata.comment: This table contains flash loan transactions across various lending protocols. Flash loans are a feature designed for developers, due to the technical knowledge required to execute one. Flash Loans allow you to borrow any available amount of assets without providing any collateral, as long as the liquidity is returned to the protocol within one block transaction.

BLOCK_NUMBER.name: BLOCK_NUMBER
BLOCK_NUMBER.comment: Also known as block height. The block number, which indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of each new block.

BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.name: BLOCK_TIMESTAMP
BLOCK_TIMESTAMP.comment: The date and time at which the block was produced.

TX_HASH.name: TX_HASH

Note: A rule is applied to nullify the `amount_USD` if there is a significant divergence between `amount_in_USD` and `amount_out_usd`, which can occur during high price fluctuations for less liquid tokens.

metadata.name: EZ_DECODED_EVENT_LOGS
metadata.comment:
For information on how to submit a contract for decoding, as well as how ABIs are sourced, please visit [here](https://science.flipsidecrypto.xyz/abi-requestor/).
      This model contains decoded event logs for contracts that we have an ABI for. Please note, this table does not include all event logs, only those that we have an ABI for.
      The `decoded_log` column is the easiest place to query decoded data. It is a JSON object, where the keys are the names of the event parameters, and the values are the values of the event parameters.
      You can select from this column using the following sample format, `decoded_log:from::string` or more generally, `decoded_log:<event_param>::datatype`. See below for a full sample query.
      The `full_decoded_logs` column contains the same information, as well as additional fields such as the datatype of the decoded data. You may need to laterally flatten this column to query the data.
            
      Sample query for USDC Transfer events:
      
      ```sql
      select 
      tx_hash,
      block_number,
      contract_address,
      decoded_log:from::string as from_address,
      decoded_log:to::string as to_address,
      decoded_log:value::integer as value
      from ethereum.core.fact_decoded_event_logs
      where contract_address = lower('0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48')
      and block_number between 16400000 and 16405000
      and event_name = 'Transfer'
      limit 50```

metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a user withdraws liquidity across various lending protocols on this EVM blockchain. Users need to make sure there is enough liquidity (not borrowed) in order to withdraw, if this is not the case, users need to wait for more liquidity from depositors or borrowers repaying. Some protocols required trace-level curation; as a result, certain columns exclusive to event logs, such as event index, will contain NULL values.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_LIQUIDATIONS
metadata.comment: This table contains transactions in which a borrower's collateral asset is liquidated across various lending protocols. Liquidations occur when a borrower's health factor goes below 1 due to their collateral value not properly covering their loan/debt value. This might happen when the collateral decreases in value or the borrowed debt increases in value against each other. This collateral vs loan value ratio is shown in the health factor. In a liquidation, up to 50% of a borrower's debt is repaid and that value + liquidation fee is taken from the collateral available, so after a liquidation the amount liquidated from one's debt is repaid.

metadata.name: EZ_LENDING_DEPOSITS
metadata.comment: This table contains deposit transactions across various lending protocols on this EVM blockchain. A user deposits their preferred asset and amount. After depositing, users earn passive income based on the market borrowing demand. Additionally, depositing allows users to borrow by using their deposited assets as collateral. Any interest earned by depositing funds helps offset the interest rate accumulated by borrowing. Some protocols required trace-level curation; as a result, certain columns exclusive to event logs, such as event index, will contain NULL values.
